projectDescription: A Python library that compresses long LLM context using a learned token eviction policy — smaller prompts, retained answer quality, and documented environmental impact.
keyFeatures: Learned ~5K-parameter MLP eviction policy that outperforms truncation on oracle recall
100% oracle recall at 35% token budget — retains answer-bearing lines missed by head/tail truncation
In-process CPU inference — sub-millisecond overhead before LLM call
Multiple baseline policies (FIFO, Truncation, H2O, Summarization) for comparison
Documented environmental impact — tokens saved → GPU-seconds → Wh → CO₂
Hosted API with Firebase auth, API key management, and per-key usage tracking
Browser-portable model via compress-engine.js for client-side demos
Integrations for OpenAI messages, LangChain history, raw pipelines, and curl
publicApiSurface: compress_context(text, question, budget_ratio, policy, checkpoint) -> CompressResult
compare_policies(text, question, budget_ratio, checkpoint) -> dict[str, CompressResult]
compress_detailed(text, question, budget_ratio, policy, checkpoint) -> (CompressResult, List[LineAnnotation])
CompressResult dataclass (compressed_text, original_tokens, kept_tokens, kv_savings_pct, compression_ratio, policy_name, budget_ratio, kept_line_ratio, question)
EvictionPolicy ABC (FIFO, TruncationPolicy, H2OPolicy, SummarizationPolicy, LearnedPolicy, OraclePolicy)
GET /api/keys — list API keys (Firebase ID token)
supercompress train [--full] — CLI to train checkpoint
supercompress demo — CLI policy comparison demo
